questions.question: Who was the founder of the Mughal Empire in India?
questions.explanation: Babur, a descendant of Timur and Genghis Khan, founded the Mughal Empire in India in 1526 after the Battle of Panipat.

questions.question: Which movement was led by Mahatma Gandhi to protest against salt tax?
questions.explanation: The Dandi March of 1930 was led by Mahatma Gandhi to protest the British salt tax, symbolizing civil disobedience.

questions.question: The Partition of India in 1947 was based on which criterion?
questions.explanation: The Partition was primarily based on religious lines, dividing India into India and Pakistan along Muslim-Hindu populations.

questions.question: Who was the last Governor-General of independent India?
questions.explanation: C. Rajagopalachari served as the last Governor-General of India from 1948 to 1950 after independence.

questions.question: The Battle of Plassey was fought in which year?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Plassey took place in 1757 and marked the beginning of British dominance in India.

questions.question: Which river is the longest in India?
questions.explanation: The Indus River, originating in Tibet, is the longest river flowing through India, Pakistan, and China.

questions.question: The 'Thar Desert' is mainly located in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: The Thar Desert covers most of Rajasthan, forming part of the Great Indian Desert.

questions.question: Which resource is primarily found in the Deccan Plateau?
questions.explanation: Bauxite, the ore of aluminium, is abundantly found in the Deccan Plateau region.

questions.question: Which climate zone is characterized by high temperatures and low rainfall?
questions.explanation: Desert climate zones have high temperatures during the day and very low rainfall, typical of the Thar Desert.

questions.question: Who appoints the Chief Justice of India?
questions.explanation: The Chief Justice of India is appointed by the President of India.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the Union Parliament?
questions.explanation: Article 81 of the Indian Constitution deals with the composition of the Parliament of India.

questions.question: The 'Directive Principles of State Policy' are found in which part of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Part IV of the Indian Constitution contains the Directive Principles of State Policy.

questions.question: Which of the following is a fundamental duty under the Indian Constitution?
options: To protect and improve the natural environment
questions.explanation: All listed options are fundamental duties listed under Article 51-A of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) is measured by which entity?
options: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation
questions.explanation: India's GDP is primarily estimated and reported by the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation.

questions.question: The 'Fiscal Deficit' of a government is the difference between:
questions.explanation: Fiscal deficit is the difference between the total government expenditure and total revenue (excluding borrowings).

questions.question: Which scheme was launched to support 'Digital India'?
questions.explanation: DigiLocker is a key component of Digital India aimed at promoting paperless governance.

questions.question: India's Goods and Services Tax (GST) was implemented in which year?
questions.explanation: GST was implemented in India on July 1, 2016, unifying indirect taxation.

questions.question: Which particle is responsible for carrying the current in a metal wire?
questions.explanation: Electrons are responsible for electrical conduction in metals.

questions.question: Which chemical element has the highest melting point?
questions.explanation: Tungsten has the highest melting point among all elements.

questions.question: Which organ in the human body is primarily responsible for detoxification?
questions.explanation: The liver plays a central role in detoxification and metabolism.

questions.question: What is the primary function of chlorophyll in plants?
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll absorbs sunlight, facilitating the process of photosynthesis.

questions.question: Which is the world's largest biodiversity hotspot?
questions.explanation: The Amazon Rainforest is the largest biodiversity hotspot in the world.

questions.question: Which international agreement aims to reduce greenhouse gas emissions?
questions.explanation: The Kyoto Protocol is an international treaty to reduce emissions of greenhouse gases.

questions.question: Which national park is known as the 'Eco-Tiger Reserve' in India?
questions.explanation: Jim Corbett National Park is renowned for tiger conservation and eco-tourism.

questions.question: Which law regulates environmental protection in India?
questions.explanation: All listed laws contribute to environmental regulation in India.

questions.question: Who is the current President of India?
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu has been serving as the President of India since 25 July 2022.

questions.question: Who is the current Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Narendra Modi has been serving as Prime Minister since 26 May 2014.

questions.question: The 'Khelo India' scheme is associated with which sector?
questions.explanation: 'Khelo India' is a national program aimed at promoting sports among youth.

questions.question: Who is the current External Affairs Minister of India?
questions.explanation: S. Jaishankar has been serving as External Affairs Minister since 2019.

questions.question: Who is the current Chief Minister of Odisha?
questions.explanation: Mohan Charan Majhi is the current Chief Minister of Odisha since 12 June 2024.
